positive_findings: run2_8_full uses stronger hierarchy and cleaner layout than prompt-only, Run 1.5, and bad-memory controls
one hero object carries the climax instead of equal panels
bad-memory-schema shows the intended negative-control failure mode: muted palette, weaker delta, and less credible climax
risks: small body text and diagram labels are hard to inspect at contact-sheet scale
high-contrast red/dark palette may overstate improvement if not tested with alternate visual directions
native render inspection and human approval are still missing

positive_findings: progression from Run 2.0 to Run 2.8 is visible
Run 2.8 shifts the series toward more concrete data-flow and executable-memory mapping
Run 2.8 keeps public-blocked release gates visible
risks: the image is too wide for reliable small-text review
some earlier run-to-run differences remain subtle at series scale
native PPT serialization/render inspection is not yet proven in public-release conditions

blocking_reasons: native or cross-platform render inspection is not complete
source-brand sanitization approval is not recorded
contact-sheet review still flags small-text legibility risk
public-demo-grade typography and final visual direction are not yet proven
next_required_action: Use the gate findings as input for the next same-stage data/workflow and design-memory thickening pass.
